text: Many experts predict that artificial intelligence will continue to advance rapidly over the next few decades. A key milestone is the development of Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI—a type of AI that can perform any intellectual task as well as a human. While today's AI is specialised, AGI would be much more flexible. The potential benefits are huge, from solving complex medical problems to improving education and fighting climate change. However, there are also serious risks, such as job displacement and ethical concerns about control. Some researchers think AGI could arrive within 20 to 50 years, while others believe it will take much longer. Regardless, its impact will be deeply significant.
